questions.question: In a connectivity graph, what is the minimum number of edges that need to be removed to disconnect the graph?
questions.explanation: The minimum number of edges that need to be removed to disconnect the graph is 2. This is because removing 2 edges will isolate at least 2 vertices.

questions.question: If a graph has 10 vertices and each vertex is connected to every other vertex, what is the edge connectivity of the graph?
questions.explanation: Since every vertex is connected to every other vertex, the edge connectivity of the graph is 9 (the number of edges in a complete graph with 10 vertices)

questions.question: In a graph, if the removal of 3 vertices results in a connected component, what is this component called?
questions.explanation: This connected component is called a cut set. A cut set is a set of vertices whose removal results in a connected component.

questions.question: What is the minimum number of vertices that need to be removed from a graph to disconnect it?
questions.explanation: The minimum number of vertices that need to be removed to disconnect the graph is 1. This is because removing 1 vertex will isolate at least 1 vertex.

questions.question: In a graph, if the removal of 2 edges results in a disconnected component, what is this connected component called?
questions.explanation: This disconnected component is called a cut vertex. A cut vertex is a vertex whose removal results in a disconnected component.
